title: Command Lifecycle, Configuration, Pipeline, and Atomic Output · Runtime Lens: Config Precedence
summary: The script reads arguments during import, mixes parsing with writes, partially replaces the public directory on failure, and cannot explain which configuration won. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the config precedence increment in import-safe deterministic build pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.
Keep import-safe definitions separate from a main entry point that returns meaningful status and writes diagnostics to the correct stream.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-cli-pipeline-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-cli-pipeline-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_cli_pipeline_2(stages: int = 10, completed: int = 10, import_safe: bool = True, staged_output: bool = True, prior_artifact_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if stages < 8 or completed < 0:
        return False, completed, "pipeline-shape"
    if completed != stages:
        return False, completed, "partial-pipeline"
    if not import_safe or not staged_output:
        return False, completed, "effect-boundary"
    if not prior_artifact_kept:
        return False, completed, "destructive-output"
    assert completed == stages
    return True, completed, "atomic-pipeline"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Config Precedence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 4221 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Config Precedence model · Theory Config Precedence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Config Precedence model · Theory Config Precedence, keep Config Precedence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Config Precedence model · Theory Config Precedence, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Config Precedence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Config Precedence model · Theory Config Precedence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 4221 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Config Precedence model · Theory Config Precedence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: The last value loaded is automatically the correct configuration.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-config-precedence-vr5ydk.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-config-precedence-vr5ydk(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_config_precedence_vr5ydk\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stages < 8 or completed < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?completed != stages)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not import_safe or not staged_output)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not prior_artifact_kept)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
